title: tetragonia haworthii
intro: Tetragonia haworthii, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōkiekie, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand and Australia, belonging to the family Aizoaceae. The leaves of Tetragonia haworthii contain medicinal properties that have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues, due to its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe and heal skin irritations, as well as its ability to support digestive health and boost the immune system. Tetragonia haworthii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusing the leaves in hot water, or used as a leafy green vegetable in salads and other dishes.
